title: Graphicraft
description: Lightweight modpack that can run on almost any computer, shaders optional!
body:
# Graphicraft!
This is a lightweight modpack based on Simply Optimized that improves the world generation and makes the graphics of Minecraft look better. It's a speedy way to get all the mods you might want to improve the vanilla experience!

## Minimum and Recommended Specs

### Minimum (untested but it should run fine)
* CPU: AMD Ryzen 5 3500/Intel Core i5-10400
* GPU: AMD Radeon RX 5700/Nvidia RTX 2060/Intel Arc A580
* RAM: 16GB DDR4

### Recommended (this is essentially my build)
* CPU: AMD Ryzen 5 5600X/Intel Core i5-12400
* GPU: AMD Radeon RX 7600 XT/Nvidia RTX 4060/Intel Arc B570
* RAM: 16GB DDR4/DDR5

## Shaderpack Guide
* Bliss - decently performant and looks very good.
* BSL - decently performant and looks good.
* Complementary - decently performant and looks good. Reimagined is vanilla+, unbound is realistic.
* Glimmer - decently performant and looks okay.
* MakeUp - very performant and looks okay.
* OPAL - not very performant and looks good.
* Photon - pretty performant and looks very good.
* Solas - not very performant and looks very good.
* Visual Vibrance - very performant and looks okay.

For recommended specs, I would recommend Bliss, Complementary, Photon, and maybe Solas.

For minimum specs, I would recommend Makeup, Photon, and maybe Visual Vibrance.

## Version Numbers
Major version number updates on a major Minecraft update or when a terrain mod is changed, minor version number updates when adding/removing many mods, and patch version number updates when adding/removing one or two mods.

## Link(s)
Bliss Shaders from: https://github.com/X0nk/Bliss-Shader/tree/Stable
